edges.description: Impaired CEBPA activity prevents normal granulocytic maturation.
Arrested myeloblasts expand and replace normal hematopoiesis.
edges.target: Bone Marrow and Blood Blast Accumulation

edges.description: Blast expansion suppresses normal hematopoiesis and causes cytopenias.
edges.source: Bone Marrow and Blood Blast Accumulation

edges.description: Reduced mature blood-cell production causes AML presentation symptoms.

nodes.description: Marrow replacement by leukemic blasts produces anemia, thrombocytopenia, and neutropenia, causing fatigue, bleeding, and infection susceptibility.

evidence.reference_title: [Pancytopenia associated to acute myeloid leukemia: a case report of aleukemic leukemia].
evidence.snippet: Bone marrow aspirate showed infiltration by 45% of myeloblasts and myelodysplasia.

nodes.description: Leukemic myeloblasts accumulate in bone marrow and may circulate in blood, meeting the morphologic framework of AML and suppressing normal erythroid, granulocytic, and megakaryocytic output.
nodes.id: Bone Marrow and Blood Blast Accumulation

evidence.reference_title: Acute myeloid leukemia: advances in diagnosis and classification.
evidence.snippet: Acute myeloid leukemia is an aggressive myeloid neoplasm characterized by ≥20% myeloblasts in the blood or bone marrow.

nodes.description: Somatic CEBPA mutations disrupt the activity of CCAAT/enhancer-binding protein alpha, a transcription factor that maintains the balance between myeloid progenitor proliferation and differentiation. Pathogenic disruption weakens the normal transcriptional program needed for granulocytic maturation.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the bZip region of the CEBPA gene: A novel prognostic factor in patients with acute myeloid leukemia.
evidence.snippet: Among them, the CCAAT-enhancer-binding protein alpha (CEBPA) plays a crucial role in maintaining the balance between myeloid proliferation and differentiation.

nodes.description: Cytopenias from marrow failure cause the major presenting complications of AML, including pancytopenia, fatigue from anemia, bleeding from thrombocytopenia, and infection risk from neutropenia.

evidence.reference_title: [Pancytopenia associated to acute myeloid leukemia: a case report of aleukemic leukemia].
evidence.snippet: AML that is presented as pancytopenia should be considered in the evaluation of marrow failure syndrome.

nodes.description: Impaired CEBPA-mediated transcription favors arrested myeloid maturation. Immature blasts persist in an undifferentiated state and begin to accumulate in marrow and peripheral blood.

evidence.reference_title: Mutations in the bZip region of the CEBPA gene: A novel prognostic factor in patients with acute myeloid leukemia.
evidence.snippet: Imbalances in this network can lead to disrupted differentiation and contribute to the development of malignant diseases.
